text.content:
These GM tools install themselves into your Macro Directory automatically — no setup. Look for them by name; each previews what it will do and asks before changing anything.

Consolidate Ammo

Rolls a character's duplicate ammo piles into a single stack each — for a runner who bought ammo a box at a time. Select the character's token, then run the macro. It previews what would merge and acts only after you confirm. Quantity and the paid value are summed, so nothing is lost and sell-back still refunds correctly. Piles only merge when they're genuinely the same: a 10-round box and a 50-round belt stay separate, because for ammo the cost is the price of the whole bundle.

Repair Stale Implants

Fixes implants that were installed before a system update added their mechanics. Foundry copies a compendium item onto a character when you drag it and never updates that copy afterwards — so bone lacing installed before its unarmed-Power field existed, or Enhanced Articulation from before its +1-die field, silently does nothing. This walks every character, previews the fixes, and writes only after you confirm.

It is safe: it fills a field only when the character's copy still holds the default and the compendium has a real value, so it never changes anything you have edited. Implants on unlinked tokens aren't touched — link the token, or edit those by hand.

The other GM macros

Award Karma, Award Nuyen, Team Karma Pool, Refresh Karma Pool, Reset Condition Monitors and Request a Skill Roll install the same way. All are GM-only.

Running from the console instead

Every macro above is a thin wrapper over the system's scripting API, which you can also call from the console (F12) or your own macros:

  • game.sr2e.consolidateAmmo(actor, { dryRun: true }) — preview; drop dryRun to act. Omit actor to use the selected token.
  • game.sr2e.repairStaleImplants() — preview (dry run); { apply: true } to write.
